summary_en: FY3/26 final dividend raised to 22 yen/share from prior forecast of 18 yen (+4 yen hike). Combined with interim dividend of 20 yen (including 2 yen special commemoration), annual dividend reaches 42 yen. Payout ratio 50.5%, DOE 4.3%. Policy change adds DOE floor of 4.0% alongside prior 50%+ consolidated payout ratio threshold.
title_en: Notice of Revision of Dividend Policy and Revision of Dividend Forecast (Dividend Increase) for Fiscal Year Ended March 2026
translation_en_brief: Pronexus announced on 14 May 2026 a revision of its dividend policy and an increase in its FY3/26 dividend forecast. The company modified its dividend policy to add a dividend on equity (DOE) floor of 4.0% alongside its existing consolidated payout ratio benchmark of 50%+. Under the new framework, the FY3/26 final dividend has been raised to 22 yen per share from the prior forecast of 18 yen, representing a 4 yen increase. Combined with the interim dividend of 20 yen per share (which includes 2 yen of special commemoration dividend marking the company's 95th anniversary), the full-year dividend is now 42 yen per share, yielding a payout ratio of 50.5% and DOE of 4.3%. The company stated the policy change reflects its intention to provide more stable and capital-efficient returns to shareholders while maintaining focus on cost of capital and capital discipline.
